description:
Long messages re-render every segment from scratch on each new token because segment identity is not preserved. Render cost is O(total message length) per token.

**Fix:** Introduce a `SegmentedMessage` component that splits content at structural boundaries (paragraph breaks `\n\n`, headings `#`, horizontal rules `---`) into segments. Each segment is a `React.memo` component keyed by `${segmentIndex}-${contentHash}`. Only the tail segment re-renders per token. Falls back to existing render for messages under 500 chars.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/ava/src/components/chat/SegmentedMessage.tsx` — create: segmentation logic + MemoizedSegment
- `apps/ava/src/utils/segmentMarkdown.ts` — create: pure segmentation function
- `apps/ava/src/utils/hashString.ts` — create: djb2 fast hash utility
- `apps/ava/src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx` — wire in SegmentedMessage above 500-char threshold
- `apps/ava/src/utils/segmentMarkdown.test.ts` — create: unit tests for edge cases

**Acceptance criteria:**
- React DevTools Profiler: only 1 segment re-renders per token on a 2000-char streaming message
- Output identical to non-segmented rendering (snapshot test)
- Messages under 500 chars use existing path (no segmentation overhead)
- Edge cases covered: single-segment message, code-block-only message, mid-paragraph ending
- Build passes, all tests pass

**Complexity:** large
title: Segment memoization for long streaming messages
